name: google-official-seo-guide
description: Official Google SEO guide covering search optimization, best practices, Search Console, crawling, indexing, and improving website search visibility based on official Google documentation
capabilities: Provide SEO recommendations based on official Google documentation
Help implement structured data (JSON-LD) for rich results
Explain crawling, indexing, and ranking processes
Guide on technical SEO including mobile-first indexing
Assist with Google Search Console usage and debugging
Recommend best practices for meta tags and content optimization
author: ZhanlinCui
securitySummary: This skill consists entirely of markdown documentation files containing SEO guidance from Google's official documentation. All static scanner findings are false positives: (1) 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' alerts are triggered by JSON-LD schema.org examples in documentation; (2) 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' alerts are triggered by markdown code block formatting; (3) 'Hardcoded URL' alerts are legitimate Google documentation URLs. No executable code or security risks present.
valueStatement: Users struggle to optimize their websites for Google Search without authoritative guidance. This skill provides comprehensive SEO assistance based on official Google documentation, covering crawling, indexing, structured data, and technical SEO best practices.
limitations: Does not make actual changes to user websites
Cannot access real-time Google Search data or rankings
No guaranteed ranking improvements - only follows Google guidelines
Does not replace professional SEO audit services for complex issues

useCases.title: Implement VideoObject Structured Data
useCases.description: Add proper JSON-LD schema markup to help videos appear as rich results in Google Search

useCases.title: Debug Indexing Issues
useCases.description: Identify why pages are not being indexed and provide solutions based on Google guidelines

useCases.title: Optimize for Mobile-First Indexing
useCases.description: Ensure website meets Google mobile-first requirements for better search visibility

promptTemplates.title: Basic SEO Question
promptTemplates.prompt: How do I improve my website's ranking in Google Search?

promptTemplates.title: Structured Data Implementation
promptTemplates.prompt: Help me add VideoObject structured data to my video page. The video is at https://example.com/video.mp4
promptTemplates.scenario: User wants to implement schema.org markup

promptTemplates.title: Indexing Debugging
promptTemplates.prompt: My pages are not appearing in Google Search. How do I troubleshoot indexing issues?

promptTemplates.title: Technical SEO Audit
promptTemplates.prompt: What technical SEO factors should I check for mobile-first indexing compliance?

bestPractices: Always verify recommendations with Google's official documentation
Use Google Search Console to monitor indexing and search performance
Focus on user-first content rather than keyword stuffing
antiPatterns: Do not guarantee specific ranking positions - Google algorithms change
Avoid participating in link schemes or buying links
Do not create duplicate content across multiple URLs without canonical tags

faq.question: How long does it take for Google to index my pages?
faq.answer: Indexing time varies from hours to weeks. Submit a sitemap in Google Search Console and ensure your pages are accessible via links.

faq.question: What is the most important ranking factor?
faq.answer: Google uses many factors. Focus on: relevant content, mobile-friendliness, page speed, secure HTTPS, and authoritative backlinks.

faq.question: Do I need to use structured data?
faq.answer: Structured data is not a ranking factor but helps Google understand your content and can enable rich results. It is recommended for certain content types.

faq.question: How do I check if my pages are indexed?
faq.answer: Use the site:yourdomain.com search operator or check Google Search Console Coverage report.

faq.question: What is mobile-first indexing?
faq.answer: Google primarily uses the mobile version of content for indexing and ranking. Ensure your mobile site has the same content as desktop.

faq.question: Should I use www or non-www URLs?
faq.answer: Choose one format and set up 301 redirects and canonical tags. Be consistent across your site.
